id: Ondernemers erkennen cyberrisico vooral pas na schade__a9-hybrid-small-to-big__0000
text: Entrepreneurs recognise cyber risk mainly only after damage. [Report](https://www.abnamro.nl/nl/zakelijk/insights/sectoren-en-trends/rapporten/index.html). [Technology, Media & Telecom](https://www.abnamro.nl/nl/zakelijk/insights/sectoren-en-trends/technologie/index.html). ![](https://assets.abnamro.com/api/public/content/stressed-man-sitting-at-workdesk-at-office-2075654179-landscape-16x9.jpg?t=h320). 21/05/2024 • 3 minutes read. [Julia Krauwer](https://www.abnamro.nl/nl/zakelijk/insights/auteurs/julia-krauwer/index.html). An overwhelming majority of companies have already faced a cyberattack.

metadata.source_title: Entrepreneurs recognise cyber risk mainly only after damage
metadata.tags: Rapport, Technologie, Media & Telecom, Cybersecurity, Digitalisering, Sociaal ondernemen
metadata.author: Julia Krauwer
metadata.description: Entrepreneurs often underestimate cyber risks. Only after an attack and the resulting damage do they recognise the risks. Read more.
metadata.parent_text: Entrepreneurs recognise cyber risk mainly only after damage. [Report](https://www.abnamro.nl/nl/zakelijk/insights/sectoren-en-trends/rapporten/index.html). [Technology, Media & Telecom](https://www.abnamro.nl/nl/zakelijk/insights/sectoren-en-trends/technologie/index.html). ![](https://assets.abnamro.com/api/public/content/stressed-man-sitting-at-workdesk-at-office-2075654179-landscape-16x9.jpg?t=h320). 21/05/2024 • 3 minutes read. [Julia Krauwer](https://www.abnamro.nl/nl/zakelijk/insights/auteurs/julia-krauwer/index.html). An overwhelming majority of companies have already faced a cyberattack. Yet entrepreneurs tend to underestimate the associated risks. Often only after an organisation has suffered damage, for example lost revenue or financial costs for system recovery, do they recognise the risks. Last year, almost three-quarters of Dutch companies faced a cyberattack, according to a survey by ABN AMRO and research agency MWM2 among 895 organisations.

id: Ondernemers erkennen cyberrisico vooral pas na schade__a9-hybrid-small-to-big__0005
text: Among large corporates, with annual turnover of at least 25 million, this share is highest at 86 per cent. In small and medium-sized enterprises (SMEs), 71 per cent were confronted with an attack last year.

metadata.chunk_id: Ondernemers erkennen cyberrisico vooral pas na schade__a9-hybrid-small-to-big__0005
metadata.source_slug: Ondernemers erkennen cyberrisico vooral pas na schade
metadata.source_title: Entrepreneurs recognise cyber risk mainly only after damage
metadata.tags: Rapport, Technologie, Media & Telecom, Cybersecurity, Digitalisering, Sociaal ondernemen
metadata.author: Julia Krauwer
metadata.description: Entrepreneurs often underestimate cyber risks. Only after an attack and the resulting damage do they recognise the risks. Read more.
metadata.parent_text: Among large corporates, with annual turnover of at least 25 million, this share is highest at 86 per cent. In small and medium-sized enterprises (SMEs), 71 per cent were confronted with an attack last year. More than half of the self-employed (55 per cent) were targeted. The study included 232 large companies, 524 SMEs and 139 self-employed professionals. The likelihood that a company will at some point be the target of a cyberattack is now particularly high; 95 per cent of large corporates have already experienced this at least once, compared with 85 per cent of SMEs and 65 per cent of sole traders. Although this percentage logically increases each year for all company sizes, this is not the case across the board for risk perception. In both the SME and sole-trader segments, the share of businesses that see cybercrime as “a lot” or “a great deal of risk” for the organisation has even fallen slightly. Respectively, 38 and 13 per cent rate the risks as high this year. This applies to a much higher share of large corporates: 60 per cent, compared with 53 last year.

metadata.source_title: Entrepreneurs recognise cyber risk mainly only after damage
metadata.tags: Rapport, Technologie, Media & Telecom, Cybersecurity, Digitalisering, Sociaal ondernemen
metadata.author: Julia Krauwer
metadata.description: Entrepreneurs often underestimate cyber risks. Only after an attack and the resulting damage do they recognise the risks. Read more.
metadata.parent_text: Among large corporates, with annual turnover of at least 25 million, this share is highest at 86 per cent. Although this percentage logically increases each year for all company sizes, this is not the case across the board for risk perception. id: Ondernemers erkennen cyberrisico vooral pas na schade__a9-hybrid-small-to-big__0014
text: Higher risk assessment after damage. Cyberattacks have now led to damage at 59 per cent of large corporates and 43 per cent of SMEs, such as financial costs for restoring systems, lost income due to business downtime or reputational damage from leaked customer data.

metadata.chunk_id: Ondernemers erkennen cyberrisico vooral pas na schade__a9-hybrid-small-to-big__0014
metadata.source_slug: Ondernemers erkennen cyberrisico vooral pas na schade
metadata.source_title: Entrepreneurs recognise cyber risk mainly only after damage
metadata.tags: Rapport, Technologie, Media & Telecom, Cybersecurity, Digitalisering, Sociaal ondernemen
metadata.author: Julia Krauwer
metadata.description: Entrepreneurs often underestimate cyber risks. Only after an attack and the resulting damage do they recognise the risks. Read more.
metadata.parent_text: Higher risk assessment after damage. Cyberattacks have now led to damage at 59 per cent of large corporates and 43 per cent of SMEs, such as financial costs for restoring systems, lost income due to business downtime or reputational damage from leaked customer data. This occurs significantly less in the sole-trader population, where only 6 per cent report ever having suffered damage from a cyberattack. Further analysis shows that companies that have actually suffered damage from an attack on average assess the risks as higher. Of these, 63 per cent see cybercrime as (very) high risk for the organisation. Of the companies that have not yet suffered damage from an attack, only 26 per cent share this risk assessment. This is comparable to the risk assessment of companies that have never been attacked at all. Even so, CEO Maarten Roerink of cybersecurity service provider MMOX also sees that experiences of similar companies can serve as a wake-up call. “Entrepreneurs who hear from their neighbour on the business park about an unpleasant cyberattack are more inclined to start looking at solutions.

id: Ondernemers erkennen cyberrisico vooral pas na schade__a9-hybrid-small-to-big__0015
text: Higher risk assessment after damage. Cyberattacks have now led to damage at 59 per cent of large corporates and 43 per cent of SMEs, such as financial costs for restoring systems, lost income due to business downtime or reputational damage from leaked customer data. This occurs significantly less in the sole-trader population, where only 6 per cent report ever having suffered damage from a cyberattack.

text:
They do not walk away with a contract straight away, but they are at least more aware of the risks.”
Measures to reduce risks. Entrepreneurs would do well to protect themselves against cyberattacks.

id: Ondernemers erkennen cyberrisico vooral pas na schade__a9-hybrid-small-to-big__0024
text: You can do this in three relatively simple steps.

metadata.chunk_id: Ondernemers erkennen cyberrisico vooral pas na schade__a9-hybrid-small-to-big__0024
metadata.source_slug: Ondernemers erkennen cyberrisico vooral pas na schade
metadata.source_title: Entrepreneurs recognise cyber risk mainly only after damage
metadata.tags: Rapport, Technologie, Media & Telecom, Cybersecurity, Digitalisering, Sociaal ondernemen
metadata.author: Julia Krauwer
metadata.description: Entrepreneurs often underestimate cyber risks. Only after an attack and the resulting damage do they recognise the risks. Read more.
metadata.parent_text: You can do this in three relatively simple steps.

id: Ondernemers erkennen cyberrisico vooral pas na schade__a9-hybrid-small-to-big__0025
text: The first is to carry out a risk analysis to map what is crucial to keep the business running and services in order. The second step is to take measures, such as promoting safe behaviour among employees, recording who owns certain data and discussing cyber risks with partners and suppliers.

metadata.chunk_id: Ondernemers erkennen cyberrisico vooral pas na schade__a9-hybrid-small-to-big__0025
metadata.source_slug: Ondernemers erkennen cyberrisico vooral pas na schade
metadata.source_title: Entrepreneurs recognise cyber risk mainly only after damage
metadata.tags: Rapport, Technologie, Media & Telecom, Cybersecurity, Digitalisering, Sociaal ondernemen
metadata.author: Julia Krauwer
metadata.description: Entrepreneurs often underestimate cyber risks. Only after an attack and the resulting damage do they recognise the risks. Read more.
metadata.parent_text: The first is to carry out a risk analysis to map what is crucial to keep the business running and services in order. The second step is to take measures, such as promoting safe behaviour among employees, recording who owns certain data and discussing cyber risks with partners and suppliers. Finally, it is essential to develop procedures to detect and handle cyber incidents.

id: Ondernemers erkennen cyberrisico vooral pas na schade__a9-hybrid-small-to-big__0026
text: The first is to carry out a risk analysis to map what is crucial to keep the business running and services in order. The second step is to take measures, such as promoting safe behaviour among employees, recording who owns certain data and discussing cyber risks with partners and suppliers. Finally, it is essential to develop procedures to detect and handle cyber incidents.
